revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:宗像]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

宗像（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]]または宗像市と[[宗像郡]]或いは宗像市とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像神郡の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]の半分（[[若宮町]]・[[宮田町]]）と[[遠賀郡]]の[[遠賀川]]まで（[[芦屋町]]・[[水巻町]]の一部を含む）と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町]]である。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と[[津屋崎町]]と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。

revision.text: 宗像（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]]または宗像市と[[宗像郡]]或いは宗像市とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像神郡の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]の半分（[[若宮町]]・[[宮田町]]）と[[遠賀郡]]の[[遠賀川]]まで（[[芦屋町]]・[[水巻町]]の一部を含む）と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]である。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と[[津屋崎町]]と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。

'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]]または宗像市と[[宗像郡]]或いは宗像市とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像神郡の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]の半分（[[若宮町]]・[[宮田町]]）と[[遠賀郡]]の[[遠賀川]]まで（[[芦屋町]]・[[水巻町]]の一部を含む）と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]である。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と[[津屋崎町]]と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。

'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]]または宗像市と[[宗像郡]]或いは宗像市とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像[[神郡]]の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]の半分（[[若宮町]]・[[宮田町]]）と[[遠賀郡]]の[[遠賀川]]まで（[[芦屋町]]・[[水巻町]]の一部を含む）と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]である。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と[[津屋崎町]]と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。 またこの地域は、古来からアジアとの繫がりは深く、[[支那人]]や[[朝鮮人]]などとの貿易が活発だったことも有名である。

'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]]または宗像市と[[宗像郡]]或いは宗像市とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像[[神郡]]の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]の半分（[[若宮町]]・[[宮田町]]）と[[遠賀郡]]の[[遠賀川]]まで（[[芦屋町]]・[[水巻町]]の一部を含む）と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]である。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と[[津屋崎町]]と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。 またこの地域は、古来からアジアとの繫がりは深く、[[中国]]や[[朝鮮]]などとの貿易が活発だったことも有名である。

'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]]または宗像市と[[宗像郡]]或いは宗像市とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像[[神郡]]の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]の半分（[[若宮町]]・[[宮田町]]）と[[遠賀郡]]の[[遠賀川]]まで（[[芦屋町]]・[[水巻町]]の一部を含む）と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]である。[[戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市郡・遠賀郡と[[中間市]]の遠賀川より西・鞍手郡である。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と[[津屋崎町]]と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。 またこの地域は、古来から[[東アジア]]（[[支那大陸]]・[[朝鮮半島]]）との繫がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。

# [[宗像市]]が市制施行するまでの[[宗像郡]]の範囲。 # [[宗像市]]の略称名。 # 広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲。（本稿で詳述）。 # [[戦国大名]]・[[宗像氏]]をはじめとする[[日本人]]の[[姓]]の一つ。 ---- '''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像[[神郡]]の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]全域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市郡・遠賀郡と[[中間市]]の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も大名である[[麻生氏]]が宗像氏との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していた為、宗像と呼んで差し支えは無い）。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。 またこの地域は、古来から[[支那大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繫がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。
[[Category:福岡県|むなかた]]

# [[宗像市]]が市制施行するまでの[[宗像郡]]の範囲。 # [[宗像市]]の略称名。 # 広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲。（本稿で詳述）。 # [[戦国大名]]・[[宗像氏]]をはじめとする[[日本人]]の[[姓]]の一つ。 ---- '''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像[[神郡]]の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]全域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市郡・遠賀郡と[[中間市]]の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も大名である[[麻生氏]]が宗像氏との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していた為、宗像と呼んで差し支えは無い）。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。 またこの地域は、古来から[[支那大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繫がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。
[[Category:福岡県|むなかた]] [[Category:宗像地方|むなかた]]

# [[宗像市]]が市制施行するまでの[[宗像郡]]の範囲。 # [[宗像市]]の略称名。 # 広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲。（本稿で詳述）。 # [[戦国大名]]・[[宗像氏]]をはじめとする[[日本人]]の[[姓]]の一つ。 ---- '''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す。郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像神郡の範囲が広域宗像である。宗像[[神郡]]の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]全域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町 (福岡県)|新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市郡・遠賀郡と[[中間市]]の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も大名である[[麻生氏]]が宗像氏との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していた為、宗像と呼んで差し支えは無い）。ただし、歴史上では宗像家の領土を指す事が多く最後まで一貫して宗像家の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く[[福間町]]と若宮町・宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]である。 またこの地域は、古来から[[支那大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繫がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。

revision.text:
{{otheruses|拡大解釈による広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲|その他}}
'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す地名。
郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像郡に鞍手郡北西部（宮若市西部域・鞍手町）の範囲が古代の広域宗像である。古代の広域宗像の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]西部地域・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]一部地域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市・福津市・遠賀郡の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も豪族である[[麻生氏]]が[[宗像氏]]との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していた為、支配地域として宗像と呼ぶこともあった）。ただし、歴史上では宗像氏の領土を指すことが多く、最後まで一貫して宗像氏の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く福津市と旧若宮町・旧宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]のごく一部である。
またこの地域は、古来から[[中国大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繫がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。
{{DEFAULTSORT:むなかた}} [[Category:宗像地方| ]] [[Category:福岡県の地名]] [[category:宗像氏|* ]]

{{otheruses|拡大解釈による広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲|その他}}

またこの地域は、古来から[[中国大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繫がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。
{{DEFAULTSORT:むなかた}} [[Category:宗像地方]] [[category:宗像氏|* ]]

{{otheruses|拡大解釈による広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲|その他}}
'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す地名。
郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像郡に鞍手郡北西部（宮若市西部・鞍手町）の範囲が古代の広域宗像である。古代の広域宗像の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]西部地域・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]一部地域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市・福津市・遠賀郡の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も豪族である[[麻生氏]]が[[宗像氏]]との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していた為、支配地域として宗像と呼ぶこともあった）。ただし、歴史上では宗像氏の領土を指すことが多く、最後まで一貫して宗像氏の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く福津市と旧若宮町・旧宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]のごく一部である。
またこの地域は、古来から[[中国大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繫がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。

{{otheruses|拡大解釈による広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲|その他}}
'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す地名。
郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像郡に鞍手郡北西部（宮若市西部・鞍手町）の範囲が古代の広域宗像である。古代の広域宗像の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]西部地域・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]一部地域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市・福津市・遠賀郡の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も豪族である[[麻生氏]]が[[宗像氏]]との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していたため、支配地域として宗像と呼ぶこともあった）。ただし、歴史上では宗像氏の領土を指すことが多く、最後まで一貫して宗像氏の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く福津市と旧若宮町・旧宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]のごく一部である。
またこの地域は、古くから[[中国大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繋がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。
{{DEFAULTSORT:むなかた}} [[Category:宗像地方]] [[category:宗像氏|*]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:宗像]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:宗像]][[Category:qq]]

{{otheruses|Broad interpretation of the extended area of Munakata and Munakata [[Shingun]]|Other uses}}
== Munakata (宗像) ==
'''Munakata''' (宗像) refers to the area encompassing {{ill|Munakata City|ja|宗像市}}, {{ill|Munakata District|ja|宗像郡}}, and their surrounding regions.
The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of {{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}} (western {{ill|Miyawaka|ja|宮若市}} and {{ill|Kurate|ja|鞍手町}}), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former {{ill|Onga District|ja|遠賀郡}}, and the eastern part of {{ill|Kasuyagun|ja|糟屋郡}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|古賀市}} and {{ill|Shingū|ja|新宮町}}).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町}}, the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町}}, {{ill|Okagaki|ja|岡垣町}}, and a small part of {{ill|Ongatown|ja|遠賀町}}.
This region has long had deep connections with {{ill|China|ja|中国大陸}} and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in {{ill|trade|ja|貿易}} since ancient times.
[[Category:Historical regions of Japan]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:宗像]]
[[Category:宗像地方]] [[Category:Munakata clan|*]] [[ja:宗像]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:宗像]][[Category:qq]]

== Munakata (宗像) ==
'''Munakata''' (宗像) refers to the area encompassing {{ill|Munakata City|ja|宗像市}}, {{ill|Munakata District|ja|宗像郡}}, and their surrounding regions.
The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of {{ill|Kurate District|ja|鞍手郡}} (western {{ill|Miyawaka|ja|宮若市}} and {{ill|Kurate|ja|鞍手町}}), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former {{ill|Onga District|ja|遠賀郡}}, and the eastern part of {{ill|Kasuyagun|ja|糟屋郡}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|古賀市}} and {{ill|Shingū|ja|新宮町}}).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町}}, the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町}}, {{ill|Okagaki|ja|岡垣町}}, and a small part of {{ill|Ongatown|ja|遠賀町}}.
This region has long had deep connections with {{ill|China|ja|中国大陸}} and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in {{ill|trade|ja|貿易}} since ancient times.
[[Category:Historical regions of Japan]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:宗像]]

The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former {{ill|Onga District|ja|遠賀郡|12=draft}}, and the eastern part of {{ill|Kasuyagun|ja|糟屋郡|12=draft}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|古賀市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shingū|ja|新宮町|12=draft}}).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|12=draft}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|12=draft}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former {{ill|Wakamiya Town|ja|若宮町|12=draft}}, the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okagaki|ja|岡垣町|12=draft}}, and a small part of {{ill|Ongatown|ja|遠賀町|12=draft}}.
This region has long had deep connections with {{ill|China|ja|中国大陸|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=draft}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in {{ill|trade|ja|貿易|12=draft}} since ancient times.

revision.text:
{{otheruses|拡大解釈による広域範囲の宗像・宗像[[神郡]]の範囲|その他}}
'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す地名。
郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像郡に鞍手郡北西部（宮若市西部・鞍手町）の範囲が古代の広域宗像である。古代の広域宗像の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]西部地域・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]一部地域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市・福津市・遠賀郡の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も豪族である[[麻生氏]]が[[宗像氏]]との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していたため、支配地域として宗像と呼ぶこともあった）。ただし、歴史上では宗像氏の領土を指すことが多く、最後まで一貫して宗像氏の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く福津市と旧若宮町・旧宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]のごく一部である。
またこの地域は、古くから[[中国大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繋がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。
{{DEFAULTSORT:むなかた}} [[Category:宗像地方]] [[category:宗像氏|*]]

'''宗像'''（むなかた）とは、[[宗像市]][[宗像郡|郡]]とその郊外の事を指す地名。
郊外の範囲も明確ではないが、[[飛鳥時代]]の宗像郡に鞍手郡北西部（宮若市西部・鞍手町）の範囲が古代の広域宗像である。古代の広域宗像の範囲は、宗像市郡と[[鞍手郡]]北西部（[[宮若市]]西部地域・[[鞍手町]]）と旧[[遠賀郡]]一部地域と東部[[糟屋郡]]（[[古賀市]]・[[新宮町]]）である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の宗像とは現在の宗像市・福津市・遠賀郡の[[遠賀川]]より鞍手郡北西部と古賀浜を含む[[新宮海岸]]である（遠賀川以東の遠賀郡も豪族である[[麻生氏]]が[[宗像氏]]との戦争後、宗像氏に従属していたため、支配地域として宗像と呼ぶこともあった）。ただし、歴史上では宗像氏の領土を指すことが多く、最後まで一貫して宗像氏の領土だったのは、宗像市と西郷の地を除く福津市と旧若宮町・旧宮田町北部・[[岡垣町]]・[[遠賀町]]のごく一部である。
またこの地域は、古くから[[中国大陸]]や[[朝鮮半島]]との繋がりは深く、[[貿易]]が活発だったことも有名である。

{{otheruses|Broad interpretation of the extended area of Munakata and Munakata [[Shingun]]|Other uses}}
== Munakata (宗像) ==
'''Munakata''' (宗像) refers to the area encompassing [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], [[:en:Munakata District, Fukuoka|Munakata District]], and their surrounding regions.
The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of [[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]] (western [[:en:Miyawaka|Miyawaka]] and [[:en:Kurate, Fukuoka|Kurate]]), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga District]], and the eastern part of [[:en:Kasuya District, Fukuoka|Kasuyagun]] ([[:en:Koga, Fukuoka|Koga]] and [[:en:Shingū, Fukuoka|Shingū]]).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onga River|qq=}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Coast|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asō clan|qq=}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former [[:en:Wakamiya, Fukuoka|Wakamiya Town]], the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyata Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Okagaki, Fukuoka|Okagaki]], and a small part of [[:en:Onga, Fukuoka|Ongatown]].
This region has long had deep connections with [[:en:Mainland China|China]] and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in [[:en:International trade|trade]] since ancient times.

During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onga River|qq=}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Coast|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asō clan|qq=}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former [[:en:Wakamiya, Fukuoka|Wakamiya Town]], the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyata Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Okagaki, Fukuoka|Okagaki]], and a small part of [[:en:Onga, Fukuoka|Ongatown]].

The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga District]], and the eastern part of [[:en:Kasuya District, Fukuoka|Kasuyagun]] ([[:en:Koga, Fukuoka|Koga]] and [[:en:Shingū, Fukuoka|Shingū]]).

== Munakata (宗像) ==
'''Munakata''' (宗像) refers to the area encompassing [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], [[:en:Munakata District, Fukuoka|Munakata District]], and their surrounding regions.
The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of [[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]] (western [[:en:Miyawaka|Miyawaka]] and [[:en:Kurate, Fukuoka|Kurate]]), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga District]], and the eastern part of [[:en:Kasuya District, Fukuoka|Kasuyagun]] ([[:en:Koga, Fukuoka|Koga]] and [[:en:Shingū, Fukuoka|Shingū]]).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onga River|1=Onga River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642388}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Coast|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asō clan|1=Asō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677563}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former [[:en:Wakamiya, Fukuoka|Wakamiya Town]], the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyata Town|1=Miyata Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455167}}, [[:en:Okagaki, Fukuoka|Okagaki]], and a small part of [[:en:Onga, Fukuoka|Ongatown]].
This region has long had deep connections with [[:en:Mainland China|China]] and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in [[:en:International trade|trade]] since ancient times.
[[Category:completion tier 1]]
{{translated page|ja|宗像|version=98446465|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Munakata (宗像) ==
'''Munakata''' (宗像) refers to the area encompassing [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], [[:en:Munakata District, Fukuoka|Munakata District]], and their surrounding regions.
The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of [[:en:Kurate District, Fukuoka|Kurate District]] (western [[:en:Miyawaka|Miyawaka]] and [[:en:Kurate, Fukuoka|Kurate]]), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga District]], and the eastern part of [[:en:Kasuya District, Fukuoka|Kasuyagun]] ([[:en:Koga, Fukuoka|Koga]] and [[:en:Shingū, Fukuoka|Shingū]]).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onga River|1=Onga River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642388}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Coast|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asō clan|1=Asō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677563}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former [[:en:Wakamiya, Fukuoka|Wakamiya Town]], the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyata Town|1=Miyata Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455167}}, [[:en:Okagaki, Fukuoka|Okagaki]], and a small part of [[:en:Onga, Fukuoka|Ongatown]].
This region has long had deep connections with [[:en:Mainland China|China]] and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in [[:en:International trade|trade]] since ancient times.
{{translated page|ja|宗像|version=98446465|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Munakata''' (宗像) refers to the area encompassing {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata City}}, {{ill|Munakata District, Fukuoka|en|Munakata District, Fukuoka|WD=Q6935606|lt=Munakata District}}, and their surrounding regions.
The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District}} (western {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka}} and {{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|Kurate, Fukuoka|WD=Q629799|lt=Kurate}}), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156362|lt=Onga District}}, and the eastern part of {{ill|Kasuya District, Fukuoka|en|Kasuya District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206294|lt=Kasuyagun}} ({{ill|Koga, Fukuoka|en|Koga, Fukuoka|WD=Q768356|lt=Koga}} and {{ill|Shingū, Fukuoka|en|Shingū, Fukuoka|WD=Q179185|lt=Shingū}}).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onga River|1=Onga River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642388}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Coast|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asō clan|1=Asō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677563}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former {{ill|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|en|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|WD=Q1639231|lt=Wakamiya Town}}, the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyata Town|1=Miyata Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455167}}, {{ill|Okagaki, Fukuoka|en|Okagaki, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206102|lt=Okagaki}}, and a small part of {{ill|Onga, Fukuoka|en|Onga, Fukuoka|WD=Q1205554|lt=Ongatown}}.
This region has long had deep connections with {{ill|Mainland China|en|Mainland China|WD=Q19188|lt=China}} and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in {{ill|International trade|en|International trade|WD=Q178803|lt=trade}} since ancient times. {{translated page|ja|宗像|version=98446465|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Munakata (宗像) ==
'''Munakata''' (宗像) refers to the area encompassing {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata City}}, {{ill|Munakata District, Fukuoka|en|Munakata District, Fukuoka|WD=Q6935606|lt=Munakata District}}, and their surrounding regions.
The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District}} (western {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka}} and {{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|Kurate, Fukuoka|WD=Q629799|lt=Kurate}}), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156362|lt=Onga District}}, and the eastern part of {{ill|Kasuya District, Fukuoka|en|Kasuya District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206294|lt=Kasuyagun}} ({{ill|Koga, Fukuoka|en|Koga, Fukuoka|WD=Q768356|lt=Koga}} and {{ill|Shingū, Fukuoka|en|Shingū, Fukuoka|WD=Q179185|lt=Shingū}}).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onga River|1=Onga River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642388}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Coast|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asō clan|1=Asō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677563}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former {{ill|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|en|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|WD=Q1639231|lt=Wakamiya Town}}, the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyata Town|1=Miyata Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455167}}, {{ill|Okagaki, Fukuoka|en|Okagaki, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206102|lt=Okagaki}}, and a small part of {{ill|Onga, Fukuoka|en|Onga, Fukuoka|WD=Q1205554|lt=Ongatown}}.
This region has long had deep connections with {{ill|Mainland China|en|Mainland China|WD=Q19188|lt=China}} and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in {{ill|International trade|en|International trade|WD=Q178803|lt=trade}} since ancient times. {{translated page|ja|宗像|version=98446465|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The exact extent of the surrounding areas is not clearly defined, but during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, the historical Munakata District included the northwestern part of {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District|1=Kurate District, Fukuoka}} (western {{ill|Miyawaka|en|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431|lt=Miyawaka|1=Miyawaka}} and {{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|Kurate, Fukuoka|WD=Q629799|lt=Kurate|1=Kurate, Fukuoka}}), which formed the ancient broad Munakata region.
The extent of ancient Munakata covered Munakata City and District, the northwestern part of Kurate District (the western area of Miyawaka and Kurate Town), part of the former {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156362|lt=Onga District|1=Onga District, Fukuoka}}, and the eastern part of {{ill|Kasuya District, Fukuoka|en|Kasuya District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206294|lt=Kasuyagun|1=Kasuya District, Fukuoka}} ({{ill|Koga, Fukuoka|en|Koga, Fukuoka|WD=Q768356|lt=Koga|1=Koga, Fukuoka}} and {{ill|Shingū, Fukuoka|en|Shingū, Fukuoka|WD=Q179185|lt=Shingū|1=Shingū, Fukuoka}}).
During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Munakata referred to what is now Munakata City, Fukutsu City, the area east of the {{ill|Onga River|ja|遠賀川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onga River|1=Onga River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642388}} in Onga District, the northwestern part of Kurate District, and the {{ill|Shingū Coast|ja|新宮海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Coast|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which includes the Koga Beach area. Since the eastern part of Onga District was under the control of the local warlord {{ill|Asō clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asō clan|1=Q11677563|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677563}}, who submitted to the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} after their conflict, it was sometimes included in the Munakata domain as well.
However, historically, the term Munakata often referred to the lands controlled by the Munakata clan. The territories that consistently remained under Munakata clan rule included Munakata City, most of Fukutsu City except for the Saigo region, former {{ill|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|en|Wakamiya, Fukuoka|WD=Q1639231|lt=Wakamiya Town|1=Wakamiya, Fukuoka}}, the northern part of former {{ill|Miyata Town|ja|宮田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyata Town|1=Q11455167|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455167}}, {{ill|Okagaki, Fukuoka|en|Okagaki, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206102|lt=Okagaki|1=Okagaki, Fukuoka}}, and a small part of {{ill|Onga, Fukuoka|en|Onga, Fukuoka|WD=Q1205554|lt=Ongatown|1=Onga, Fukuoka}}.
This region has long had deep connections with {{ill|Mainland China|en|Mainland China|WD=Q19188|lt=China|1=Mainland China}} and the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, and it is well known for its active engagement in {{ill|International trade|en|International trade|WD=Q178803|lt=trade|1=International trade}} since ancient times. {{translated page|ja|宗像|version=98446465|comment=Imported full ja history}}
